targetDecisions.callerVerificationDispositionRationale: Repository-wide search across 22026 enumerated files (16 queries, 500 raw hits, 329 unique path/line rows, zero ambiguous rows) found MandatoryGateway/createMandatoryGateway defined and self-constructed only inside their own defining module (mandatory-gateway.ts lines 66/219/223), exercised only by the module's own co-located test file (mandatory-gateway.test.ts, 10 TEST_ONLY rows), mirrored in generated coverage HTML/XML/JSON (GENERATED_COVERAGE, non-authority), and otherwise referenced only in governance/audit/architecture-catalog/session-routing prose (HISTORICAL_DOCUMENT) or one private external AST-declaration scout report (PRIVATE_EXTERNAL_EVIDENCE). The public package surface (EXTENSIONS/CVF_GUARD_CONTRACT/package.json exports/files) and the barrel module (EXTENSIONS/CVF_GUARD_CONTRACT/src/index.ts) were independently re-verified in this T2 tranche and confirmed to omit both the module path and the symbol entirely -- corroborating, with fresh direct evidence, the same conclusion the R94-T1B tranche (2026-07-11) and T1 (2026-07-15) reached.
targetDecisions.architectureRecommendationRationale: Zero unresolved/ambiguous matches for this target, satisfying the work order's requirement for a no-caller recommendation. Proposal only: a reviewer/closer should consider a formal GC-009 no-non-test-production-caller architecture GAP entry; this worker does not create or mutate any owner/GAP record.

definitionLines.lineCiteNote: Work order cites line 129 (a known one-line citation offset already identified and accepted by the T1 closure -- line 129 is the closing brace of a preceding JSDoc block; line 130 is the actual `export class AgentExecutionRuntime {` declaration). This is a citation artifact, not a source-fact discrepancy, and does not change this target's disposition.

targetDecisions.callerVerificationDispositionRationale: Repository-wide search found AgentExecutionRuntime defined only at its own class declaration (agent-execution-runtime.ts line 130), constructed (`new AgentExecutionRuntime(`) only inside its own co-located test file and the two provider test files (agent-execution-runtime.test.ts, gemini-provider.test.ts, alibaba-dashscope-provider.test.ts -- all TEST_ONLY). The two non-test provider source files (gemini-provider.ts, alibaba-dashscope-provider.ts) import only the `ExecutionProvider` TYPE via `import type { ExecutionProvider } from '../agent-execution-runtime'` -- a type-only import, not a class construction or invocation -- plus one COMMENT_ONLY JSDoc mention each. No non-test file anywhere in the 22026-file universe constructs or invokes the AgentExecutionRuntime class. The package surface and barrel omit this module identically to GC-009 (see packageSurfaceCorroboration below, shared verification).
targetDecisions.architectureRecommendationRationale: Zero unresolved/ambiguous matches for this target. Proposal only: a reviewer/closer should consider a formal GC-010 no-non-test-production-caller architecture GAP entry, potentially combined with GC-009 per OWNER-GAP-01's grouped framing in the accepted T1 record; this worker does not create or mutate any owner/GAP record.
targetDecisions.additionalHistoricalCorroboration: Archived historical review lineage (docs/reviews/cvf_phase_governance/archive/CVF_INDEPENDENT_EXPERT_REVIEW_POST_SPRINT_2026-03-12.md and CVF_INDEPENDENT_POSTFIX_REVIEW_2026-03-12.md, both HISTORICAL_DOCUMENT/HISTORICAL_NON_AUTHORITY) explicitly documents that the /api/execute Web route bypassed AgentExecutionRuntime entirely as of 2026-03-12. This is cited as corroborating historical evidence only, not as fresh proof; /api/execute's current wiring was not re-verified because it is outside the two named symbol/module targets and the 16 authorized queries, and re-verifying it would be scope expansion beyond this work order's Required Search Method.
